untranslatable_reason: Calendar spread strategy with 'proximity to strike price' requires derivatives pricing data (option strike levels, implied volatility, time decay monitoring) and multi-leg position tracking that are not available in the standard price/technical indicator set. The signal also requires tracking the Greeks (theta decay, vega exposure) and option moneyness relative to strikes, which cannot be derived from basic OHLCV data alone.
translation_notes: MCMILLAN_DRY_399 is a theoretical concept about option spread profitability zones rather than a tradeable technical signal. It requires: (1) knowledge of specific strike prices being used, (2) real-time option Greeks (delta, gamma, theta, vega), (3) tracking of calendar spread P&L across the strike price surface. Without access to derivatives contract specifications and Greeks calculations, this cannot be implemented in a standard price-based backtest engine.
